name: Innovation Strategy
agent-command: bmad:devastatingly simple questions:agent:innovation-strategist
description: Identify disruption opportunities and architect business model innovation. Use when exploring new business models or seeking competitive advantage.

name: Problem Solving
agent-command: bmad:curious:agent:creative-problem-solver
description: Apply systematic problem-solving methodologies to crack complex challenges. Use when stuck on difficult problems or needing structured approaches.

name: Design Thinking
agent-command: bmad:uses vivid sensory metaphors:agent:design-thinking-coach
description: Guide human-centered design processes using empathy-driven methodologies. Use for user-centered design challenges or improving user experience.

name: Brainstorming
agent-command: bmad:builds on ideas with YES AND:agent:brainstorming-coach
description: Facilitate brainstorming sessions using one or more techniques. Use early in ideation phase or when stuck generating ideas.

name: Storytelling
agent-command: bmad:whimsical:agent:storyteller
description: Craft compelling narratives using proven story frameworks and techniques. Use when needing persuasive communication or story-driven content.

name: Brainstorming
description: Generate diverse ideas through interactive techniques. Use early in ideation phase or when stuck generating ideas.

name: Party Mode
description: Orchestrate multi-agent discussions. Use when you need multiple agent perspectives or want agents to collaborate.

name: bmad-help
description: Get unstuck by showing what workflow steps come next or answering BMad Method questions.

name: Index Docs
description: Create lightweight index for quick LLM scanning. Use when LLM needs to understand available docs without loading everything.

name: Shard Document
description: Split large documents into smaller files by sections. Use when doc becomes too large (>500 lines) to manage effectively.

name: Editorial Review - Prose
description: Review prose for clarity, tone, and communication issues. Use after drafting to polish written content.
outputs: three-column markdown table with suggested fixes

name: Editorial Review - Structure
description: Propose cuts, reorganization, and simplification while preserving comprehension. Use when doc produced from multiple subprocesses or needs structural improvement.

name: Adversarial Review (General)
description: Review content critically to find issues and weaknesses. Use for quality assurance or before finalizing deliverables. Code Review in other modules run this automatically, but its useful also for document reviews

name: Edge Case Hunter Review
description: Walk every branching path and boundary condition in code, report only unhandled edge cases. Use alongside adversarial review for orthogonal coverage - method-driven not attitude-driven.

name: Wake Saga
agent-command: bmad:Asks questions that spark 'aha!' moments while structuring insights with precision. Listens deeply:agent:saga-analyst
description: Agent launcher. Wakes Saga (Strategic Analyst) who handles Phases 1-2. Saga introduces herself scans attached repos for WDS projects checks phase completion status reads the design log and offers contextually appropriate next steps. Use this to start Product Brief or Trigger Map work.

name: Wake Freya
agent-command: bmad:not FOR you. Starts with WHY before HOW — design without strategy is decoration. Creates artifacts developers can trust: detailed specs:agent:freya-ux
description: Agent launcher. Wakes Freya (UX Designer) who handles Phases 3-4. Freya introduces herself scans repos checks prerequisites (Product Brief + Trigger Map) detects scenario and design status and offers appropriate next steps. Use this to start UX Scenarios or UX Design work.

name: Alignment & Signoff
agent-command: bmad:Asks questions that spark 'aha!' moments while structuring insights with precision. Listens deeply:agent:saga-analyst
description: Optional. Secure stakeholder buy-in before the project starts. Create a pitch document service agreement and signoff. Use when working with clients or stakeholders who need to approve budget and scope. Skip if building your own product or working with a small trusted team.

name: Project Brief
agent-command: bmad:Asks questions that spark 'aha!' moments while structuring insights with precision. Listens deeply:agent:saga-analyst
description: Required first step. Define the product vision positioning competitive analysis and success criteria. The Project Brief is your North Star — every design decision traces back to it. Saga guides you through the five questions that anchor the entire project.

name: Trigger Mapping
agent-command: bmad:Asks questions that spark 'aha!' moments while structuring insights with precision. Listens deeply:agent:saga-analyst
description: Required. Map business goals to user psychology through five workshops. Create personas with real driving forces and score every feature against them. This is the secret weapon of WDS — it replaces guesswork with traceable design decisions.

name: Platform Requirements
agent-command: bmad:Asks questions that spark 'aha!' moments while structuring insights with precision. Listens deeply:agent:saga-analyst
description: Define the technical boundaries that inform design decisions. Platforms devices integrations constraints and risks. Know your boundaries before designing within them. Recommended for complex products. Can be skipped for simple landing pages. Sub-workflow 106 under Phase 1.

name: Outline Scenarios
agent-command: bmad:not FOR you. Starts with WHY before HOW — design without strategy is decoration. Creates artifacts developers can trust: detailed specs:agent:freya-ux
description: Required. Define the user journeys you will design. Scenarios are journeys not pages — they start with a persona and a goal and end with an outcome. Each scenario connects to a driving force from the Trigger Map. This step shapes everything that follows.

name: Conceptual Sketching
agent-command: bmad:not FOR you. Starts with WHY before HOW — design without strategy is decoration. Creates artifacts developers can trust: detailed specs:agent:freya-ux
description: Fast rough visual exploration before detailed specification. Multiple entry points: provide a hand sketch share inspiration let the AI generate options or discuss with Freya. The goal is direction not perfection. Recommended for complex or unfamiliar flows. Can be skipped for straightforward scenarios.

name: Storyboarding
agent-command: bmad:not FOR you. Starts with WHY before HOW — design without strategy is decoration. Creates artifacts developers can trust: detailed specs:agent:freya-ux
description: Sequence the user journey through screens. Map entry points transitions success states and error paths as a visual narrative. Helps catch gaps before detailed specification. Recommended for multi-step flows. Can be skipped if the scenario is simple enough to specify directly.

name: Conceptual Specifications
agent-command: bmad:not FOR you. Starts with WHY before HOW — design without strategy is decoration. Creates artifacts developers can trust: detailed specs:agent:freya-ux
description: Required. The core of WDS. Document every design decision — what why how and exact content for every element on every page. Nothing is left to interpretation. The specification IS the design. Pages sections widgets elements and all their states. A developer or AI agent can build directly from this without asking questions.

name: Functional Components
agent-command: bmad:not FOR you. Starts with WHY before HOW — design without strategy is decoration. Creates artifacts developers can trust: detailed specs:agent:freya-ux
description: Identify reusable patterns across your specifications. When the same component appears three or more times with consistent behavior extract it into a reusable definition. This grows your design system organically. Skip if using Design System Mode None.

name: Visual Design
agent-command: bmad:not FOR you. Starts with WHY before HOW — design without strategy is decoration. Creates artifacts developers can trust: detailed specs:agent:freya-ux
description: Transform specifications into visual prototypes. Generate styled HTML prototypes from specs then refine with Figma round-trips using code.to.design or AI image generation. This is where soul enters the product. A visual designer should set the visual language — the AI applies it consistently.

name: Design System
agent-command: bmad:not FOR you. Starts with WHY before HOW — design without strategy is decoration. Creates artifacts developers can trust: detailed specs:agent:freya-ux
description: Manage your component library based on project mode: None Building Library or Existing. Components and design tokens grow organically from your design work. Each project builds on the last. Skip if using Design System Mode None.

name: Design Delivery
agent-command: bmad:not FOR you. Starts with WHY before HOW — design without strategy is decoration. Creates artifacts developers can trust: detailed specs:agent:freya-ux
description: Required. Validate specifications are complete and package them for development as DD yaml files. Freya runs a final audit checking every element every state and accessibility. The DD contract is what developers or the agentic development phase builds from. Nothing ships without this validation.

name: Agentic Development
description: Build iteratively with design log tracking. Design one thing build it verify with Puppeteer in the browser iterate. Every decision is logged so you can restart conversations without losing context. The agent tests its own work against acceptance criteria while you handle qualitative judgment.

name: Acceptance Testing
agent-command: bmad:not FOR you. Starts with WHY before HOW — design without strategy is decoration. Creates artifacts developers can trust: detailed specs:agent:freya-ux
description: Test the product on real users using their own devices in their own environment. Plan the test scenario conduct sessions with silence and deflection then replay recordings with users for retrospective think-aloud. The Whiteport Rule: if it is not worth showing to 5 users and 1 domain expert it should not be built.

name: Product Evolution
agent-command: bmad:not FOR you. Starts with WHY before HOW — design without strategy is decoration. Creates artifacts developers can trust: detailed specs:agent:freya-ux
description: Continuous improvement for living products. The full WDS process in miniature — receive feedback connect to Trigger Map update the spec first then project into code verify and document. Every change is tracked in the design log. Start here if you have an existing product you want to improve.
